text:
The sun peeked through the heavy clouds hanging low in the sky. It was Thursday afternoon, and Emily decided it was time to finally tackle the mountain of laundry that had been looming over her living room for weeks. She could hear the critters chirping outside their little abodes. There weren't any mosquitoes that day, just an overwhelming urge to nap.

After a quick shower and some searching online for the most earth-friendly detergent, she got to work. She could tell right away that it wouldn't be her usual cheerful afternoon. The task seemed endless, each garment a puzzle she didn't immediately recognize.

As she sorted through sock drawer after drawer, a small gray rabbit hopped beside her, sniffing at the laundry mat where freshly washed clothes lay. Emily chuckled softly. It was a pet, or maybe it was someone's pet.

Not wanting to scare it, Emily gave a gentle nudge. The rabbit turned, ears pricked. Its eyes were wide, and it squeaked cut
